what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that records a design rationale arguing that Obsidian's built-in full-text search already provides instant, offline, mobile search over the project's plain-markdown items without any custom engineering.
observations: Title frames the note as 'P4 · 1. Instant search — CLEAN. Top of the list.'
Claims Obsidian indexes file bodies plus frontmatter and runs instantly offline on mobile
States the corpus is 265 plain markdown items and therefore needs 'zero adaptation'
Explicitly characterizes the approach as read-only: 'reads bodies, never touches edges, never writes'
Uses the evaluative shorthand 'Both axes green'
Links to parent item board://item-3480c157 via part_of
Authored by claude_code in session ch-3mpkjg3r and filed on 2026-06-24
standouts: Frames using Obsidian's native search as the lowest-effort, immediately-real win
Insists on a read-only boundary: no edge touching and no writes
Uses a terse green/green evaluation without defining the axes
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses board:// URI references for linked items
Markdown body uses a numbered heading and bold emphasis for key claims
No code symbols or import/call references are present
concerns: Assertive claims about performance and mobile behavior are not substantiated in the file
Project-specific shorthand such as 'edges' and 'both axes green' is undefined here
Assumes all 265 items are plain markdown and frontmatter-searchable without qualification
Contains no implementation detail or verification steps
notes: A concise design note arguing that Obsidian's built-in full-text search already satisfies the project's instant-search need with no engineering work.
questions: What are the two axes that are 'green'?
What does 'edges' refer to in 'never touches edges'?
How current is the 265-item count and where is it sourced?
What is the parent item board://item-3480c157 and how does this note fit into it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—document why Obsidian full-text search is the top clean win—matches the content; it is a rationale/decision record rather than an implementation artifact.
intention_signals: The note appears to be lobbying for a product decision: defer or avoid building custom search and instead rely on the existing Obsidian runtime, treating the markdown corpus as already searchable.
novelty: It treats 'do nothing' as a deliberate, positive feature decision and explicitly defines a read-only safety boundary for search.
contribution: Captures a decision-record-style rationale for relying on Obsidian's native search, including the zero-adaptation argument and the read-only safety claim, which a fused system would preserve as context for search architecture choices.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item P4·1 records the rationale for using Obsidian's built-in full-text search across 265 plain markdown items as an instant, offline, mobile-ready, zero-engineering search solution.
intention_for_embedding: To justify prioritizing Obsidian's native search as the immediate, lowest-effort search capability for the project.
